summary: FREE11 Firebase Push Notification Integration tested (iteration 30). Backend 100% (15/15 tests pass). All Android TWA file structures verified. Frontend firebase.js and AuthContext integration confirmed working.
action_items: INFO: Add firebase-service-account.json to /app/backend/ to enable actual FCM push delivery (currently sent=false, MOCKED). Get JSON from Firebase Console → Project Settings → Service Accounts.
INFO: Set REACT_APP_FIREBASE_APP_ID in frontend/.env — currently empty, which silently disables web push initialization (initFirebase returns null without error).

INFO: Set REACT_APP_FIREBASE_VAPID_KEY in frontend/.env — required for web push token registration via getToken(). Get from Firebase Console → Project Settings → Cloud Messaging → Web Push certificates.
INFO: Free11MessagingService.kt uses hardcoded BACKEND_URL='https://free11.com/api/v2/push/register' — this is correct for production but would need updating for staging builds.
critical_code_review_comments: firebase.js: REACT_APP_FIREBASE_APP_ID is empty so initFirebase() always returns null (expected/intentional until app is configured). This is handled gracefully with a console.info log — no crashes.
firebase.js: getToken is imported twice — once inside initFirebase() and once inside requestAndGetToken(). The second dynamic import is redundant but harmless (module caching prevents re-fetching).
AuthContext.js: registerWebPushToken is called fire-and-forget (non-blocking) after both login() and register() — correct implementation as per spec.
fcm_service.py: send_push() returns False when no tokens registered (user has no devices) or Firebase not initialized. Both cases correctly return sent=false.
AndroidManifest.xml: Free11MessagingService exported=false (correct — should not be exportable). MESSAGING_EVENT intent-filter properly registered.

features_tested.push_register_upsert_same_device_type: PASS - Second call with same device_type updates same record
features_tested.push_register_401_invalid_token: PASS - Returns 401 with invalid bearer token
features_tested.push_register_default_device_type: PASS - Default device_type=android when not provided
features_tested.push_send_returns_sent_bool_and_target_u: PASS - Response has {sent: bool, target_user_id: string}
features_tested.push_send_sent_false_dev_mode: PASS - sent=false without Firebase service account (MOCKED expected)
features_tested.push_send_user_id_targets_specific_user: PASS - user_id param sets target_user_id in response
features_tested.android_twa_onMessageReceived_method: PASS - override fun onMessageReceived(remoteMessage: RemoteMessage) present
features_tested.android_twa_onNewToken_method: PASS - override fun onNewToken(token: String) present
features_tested.frontend_fcm_permission_denied_no_crash: PASS - console shows [FCM] Notification permission denied, no crash
features_tested.frontend_firebase_app_id_empty_no_crash: PASS - firebase.js returns null gracefully when appId empty

mocked_apis_noted: MOCKED: FCM push delivery (sent=false) — needs /app/backend/firebase-service-account.json for actual push delivery to devices
Web push disabled: REACT_APP_FIREBASE_APP_ID and REACT_APP_FIREBASE_VAPID_KEY both empty in frontend/.env

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all file structures verified, integration code correct, FCM gracefully degraded)

seed_data_creation: FCM test tokens stored in user_devices and fcm_tokens collections for admin user. Harmless test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notification integration tested (iteration 30). Backend 100% pass. Android TWA file structure complete. Frontend firebase.js and AuthContext correct. FCM delivery is MOCKED (sent=false) until firebase-service-account.json is provided. Web push needs REACT_APP_FIREBASE_APP_ID and REACT_APP_FIREBASE_VAPID_KEY set. Prev iteration 29: OTP email verification.
